text: Literally, ‘Also they shall fear what is high, and be afraid on the road. The almond-tree will flourish, the locust will grow fat, and the caper-berry will be scattered to the winds, because man is …’.

vv. 2-6: The allegory of these verses has been the subject of much dispute among commentators; but it is probable that they describe, first the loss of physical and nervous strength which accompanies old age, and then (in verse 6) the sudden accidents which may cause death. The rendering above tries to indicate the lines of the interpretation commonly given, without insisting on its details.
